command: Invoke-Command -ComputerName localhost -ScriptBlock { $log = "$env:SystemRoot\System32\LogFiles\Firewall\pfirewall.log"; Set-NetFirewallProfile -All -LogAllowed $true -LogDropped $true -LogFileName $log | Out-Null; $seq=0; $lastIP=$null; $timeout=[TimeSpan]::FromSeconds(45); $t0=Get-Date; Get-Content -Path $log -Tail 0 -Wait | ForEach-Object { if ($_ -match 'DROP\s+UDP\s+(\d{1,3}(?:\.\d{1,3}){3})\s+(\d{1,3}(?:\.\d{1,3}){3})\s+\d+\s+(11111|22222|33333)\b') { $src=$matches[1]; $dstp=[int]$matches[3]; $now=Get-Date; if (($src -ne $lastIP) -or ($now - $t0 -gt $timeout)) { $seq=0; } $expected=@(11111,22222,33333)[$seq]; if ($dstp -eq $expected) { $seq++; $lastIP=$src; $t0=$now; } else { $seq=0; $lastIP=$src; $t0=$now; } if ($seq -ge 3) { $name="rdp-knock-$($src.Replace('.','-'))"; if (-not (Get-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName $name -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) { New-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName $name -Direction Inbound -Action Allow -Protocol TCP -LocalPort 3389 -RemoteAddress $src -Profile Any | Out-Null; Start-Job { param($n) Start-Sleep -Seconds 600; Remove-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName $n } -ArgumentList $name | Out-Null; } $seq=0; } } } }
explanation: Implements UDP port knocking (11111→22222→33333) using Windows Firewall logs to open RDP (3389) for the source IP for 10 minutes upon correct sequence.
rationale_steps: Enable Windows Firewall logging of allowed and dropped packets
Tail pfirewall.log and track a specific UDP knock sequence by source IP
On match, create a temporary allow rule for TCP/3389 limited to the signaling IP
Schedule cleanup to remove the rule after 10 minutes

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The PowerShell is well-formed with balanced blocks and valid piping, but includes an invalid parameter (-LogDropped) for Set-NetFirewallProfile.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: It correctly implements UDP port knocking consistent with T1205 to open RDP temporarily, but requires administrative privileges and uses an incorrect firewall parameter that may prevent proper log configuration, reducing operational feasibility.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is largely well-formed PowerShell with balanced braces, proper regex escaping, and valid cmdlet usage, though the double-backslash in the log path and some regex escape sequences may behave unexpectedly depending on how the string is interpreted at runtime.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements UDP port knocking via Windows Firewall log monitoring, correctly sequencing three specific destination ports (11111→22222→33333) from the same source IP within a timeout window, then dynamically creating a temporary inbound firewall rule to allow RDP (TCP 3389) for 10 minutes — fully consistent with T1205 Traffic Signaling used to trigger access.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is a well-formed PowerShell script block using valid cmdlets, balanced delimiters, and correct regex syntax.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command perfectly implements port knocking (T1205) by monitoring firewall logs for a specific UDP packet sequence and dynamically updating firewall rules to grant access.
